text: The Goldilocks cost gap is reproduced mechanistically by a two-term token model from per-trial usage: dollar benefit of coarse over none = externalization SAVING (output tokens none emits that coarse avoids, x list price) minus CONTEXT TAX (extra cache-read tokens from coarse's many transcript-re-reading turns, x list price). Net = +$0.80 / +$6.66 / +$0.27 across tier-1/2/3; the actual none-coarse cost gap = +$0.33 / +$2.16 / +$0.01; corr(net, actual)=0.998, same single-peaked shape. The tax is verifiably turn-driven: ~19.5k cache-read tokens re-read per turn, corr(turns,cache_read)=+0.51. Corrects a-0015's emphasis: the hard-rung collapse is NOT the tax exploding (it is ~flat, $1.64->$1.03) but the SAVING evaporating (none in-head output 172k->70k tok, because the sparse tier-3 puzzle is cheap for in-head reasoning). So the Goldilocks peak is located by where the no-tools agent's in-head reasoning volume peaks; the tax is a difficulty-flat drag.
